what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies Suite._chat_context widens the focus plug-in to accept ui:// element addresses alongside canvas node ids, resolving registered addresses via UI_REGISTRY, marking unregistered addresses fail-loud, and preserving the original node-id co-presence block.
observations: Tests the deterministic _chat_context seam without invoking the model/fabric chat path
Constructs the focus object exactly as the frontend ships it: {selected: [...]}
Preconditions that ui://toolbar/run is genuinely present in the live UI_REGISTRY rather than using a fabricated handle
Covers registered ui:// address, unregistered ui:// address, canvas node-id, mixed selection, and no-focus cases
Uses a temporary FsStore and a small two-node graph for node-id focus tests
Expects an INDICATING block for ui:// addresses distinct from the OPERATOR'S CURRENT FOCUS co-presence block
Requires unregistered addresses to appear in context with an '(unregistered)' marker instead of being silently dropped
standouts: Fail-loud rule 4 enforcement: unregistered ui:// addresses must be retained and marked
Additive widening: the canvas-node co-presence path is preserved, not replaced
Live registry precondition check makes the test depend on real registry contents
conventions: Long docstring explains the feature seam, constraints, and test scope before imports
Acceptance test references an external seam document (seams-rhm.md Seam 4)
Uses a global PASS counter and a small check helper for labeled assertions
Cleans up temporary store directory in a finally block
Hard-codes a real UI registry address (ui://toolbar/run) to test resolution
concerns: Directly calls private method _chat_context and reads private attribute UI_REGISTRY
Global PASS counter and check helper mutate module-level state
Hard-coded registered address string makes the test brittle if the UI registry changes
Mixed-focus test does not assert relative ordering or non-overlap of the two blocks
No explicit assertion that resolved descriptions appear only in the INDICATING block
notes: An acceptance test that pins how focus.selected carries ui:// addresses from the frontend into the deterministic chat-context builder.
questions: What is the exact return contract and block formatting of Suite._chat_context?
How is UI_REGISTRY populated and what keeps ui://toolbar/run registered over time?
What other fields can the frontend focus object contain besides selected?
What should happen when focus.selected contains duplicate or partially invalid entries?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file tests the documented I1 widening of focus to accept ui:// addresses while preserving the canvas-node co-presence path.
intention_signals: Prove that the focus parameter can carry UI element addresses from the frontend and that the backend context builder resolves or fail-loud marks them without dropping the existing canvas-node co-presence path.
novelty: Tests UI-address registry integration inside a graph-suite chat-context seam, treating focus.selected as a polymorphic locus that may be canvas node ids or ui:// addresses.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance test that pins focus.selected behavior for ui:// addresses: registered addresses resolve to descriptions, unregistered addresses are included with an unregistered marker, and canvas node ids continue to produce the co-presence block.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for widening the focus parameter to accept ui:// addresses while preserving canvas node-id co-presence in Suite._chat_context.
intention_for_embedding: Verify the frontend-to-backend focus wire supports resolved registered UI addresses, fail-loud unregistered addresses, and unchanged node-id co-presence.

symbols.description: Local assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter, prints an ok message for a passing labeled condition, and raises an AssertionError with the label if the condition is false.
